b52e1067-c609-4684-9557-acc09bbd26db.text:
Non-Disclosure Agreement

This Agreement is entered into this …….(Date) day of ………….(Month), . (Year) between (Name of the Mission) (hereinafter called as "Discloser") and  (Name of the Company with Address) (hereinafter called as "Recipient"), collectively "Party" or "Parties".

512d25fe-f151-47bf-b060-fcb91adb03ab.label: text_chunk
512d25fe-f151-47bf-b060-fcb91adb03ab.text: WHEREAS the Discloser possesses certain information relating to the security set-up, security architecture, lay-out, security processes and procedures, designs, drawings, software  and  hardware  configuration,  computer  programs,  algorithms,  services, customers  etc  that  is  confidential  and  proprietary  in  nature  (hereinafter  called  as "Confidential Information"); and

fffddeb4-5860-4a58-af2d-1c6d5d2a4fb5.label: text_chunk
fffddeb4-5860-4a58-af2d-1c6d5d2a4fb5.text: WHEREAS the Recipient is bound to get to know about the Confidential Information in  pursuant  to  the  terms of  the  Agreement  for  the  purpose  of  supply,  installation, testing and commissioning of a CCTV System (hereinafter called as "Purpose") in the (hereinafter called as "Premises");

f0d5ea68-4ed3-4269-a82d-79a68dcd2e5b.label: text_chunk
f0d5ea68-4ed3-4269-a82d-79a68dcd2e5b.text:
NOW THEREFORE, in consideration for the mutual undertakings of the Discloser and the Recipient under this Agreement, the Parties agree as follows:

1.

8c8f726c-1b12-4060-972e-87a315fffdc0.label: text_chunk
8c8f726c-1b12-4060-972e-87a315fffdc0.text:
Disclosure:

 Recipient  agrees  not  to  disclose  and  the  Discloser  agrees  to  let  the Recipient  have  the  access  to  the  Confidential  Information  as  identified  and reduced in writing or provided verbally or in any other way not reduced in writing at the time of such disclosure of the information.

2. Confidentiality:

2.

0000f927-c6b8-428f-be2a-ce058ad2f2c3.label: text_chunk
0000f927-c6b8-428f-be2a-ce058ad2f2c3.text: 1 No Use: Recipient agrees not to use the Confidential Information in any way or under any circumstances share the same, in writing or through any other means, with any Third Party.2.2 No Unauthorized Disclosure: Recipient agrees to use its best efforts to prevent and protect the Confidential Information, or any part thereof, from disclosure to any person(s) or entity (ies), even if authorized or directed under any law, without the express permission of the Discloser.

bb29f674-3850-4f7b-b1ec-45cfd9d7ddbe.label: text_chunk
bb29f674-3850-4f7b-b1ec-45cfd9d7ddbe.text:
Discloser, notwithstanding, shall have the right to  deny  such  disclosure  of  the  Confidential  Information  being  detrimental  to  the security interests of the Discloser and/or its
2.3 Protection of Secrecy: Recipient agrees to take all steps necessary to protect the secrecy of the Confidential Information, and to prevent the Confidential Information from failing into the public domain or into the possession of unauthorized person(s) and/or entity(ies).2.

5a4c6d0b-1740-43f7-9e19-ad1c9ed209cf.label: text_chunk
5a4c6d0b-1740-43f7-9e19-ad1c9ed209cf.text: 4 Recipient agrees that the layout plan of the structural design of the Premises, whether  in  in  physical  or  electronic  form,  shall  always  be  in  the  custody  of  the Discloser.  However,  the  Recipient  shall  have  the  access  to  the  layout  plan  for  the purpose of carrying out the contract for installation of CCTV System.3.

b52e13bb-dc37-4d2e-9365-71b2e2d1406f.label: text_chunk
b52e13bb-dc37-4d2e-9365-71b2e2d1406f.text:
Notices: All notices hereunder shall be given by letter, addressed as follows:

4. Term  and  Termination.

 The  term  of  this  Agreement  shall  commence  on  the
Effective Date i.e. the date of signing the Agreement for the Purpose and continue for such a period until and unless the Discloser terminates the Agreement or the Premises is relocated or vacated or abandoned, whichever is earlier.

5. Breach.

92f0e00e-3e6e-409c-9fb2-ff841cecf698.label: text_chunk
92f0e00e-3e6e-409c-9fb2-ff841cecf698.text: The  Recipient  acknowledges  that  disclosure  or  use  of  Confidential Information  in  violation  of  this  Agreement  could  cause  irreparable  harm  to Discloser  including  loss  of  lives  and  limbs  of  the  persons  and  damage  to  the property, for which monetary damages may be difficult to ascertain or turn to be meaningless.

3284de2e-2ed4-4c98-8da3-2a194ffab416.label: text_chunk
3284de2e-2ed4-4c98-8da3-2a194ffab416.text:
The Recipient therefore agrees that Discloser will have the right, in addition to its other rights and remedies, to seek injunctive relief for violations of this Agreement.

6. In case the Discloser suspects any violation of this Agreement, upon reasonable notice,

 it shall be binding for the Recipient to allow the Discloser to carry out an Audit by itself or by an authorized representative. In such a situation, the Recipient shall cooperate with the Discloser.

4f33e3eb-0ea3-4e0a-9140-b55d0cb49ccc.label: text_chunk

563bea55-c53c-4ba0-97bc-c3b5e83523df.label: text_chunk
563bea55-c53c-4ba0-97bc-c3b5e83523df.text: Any dispute or difference arising out of or in connection with this Non- Discloser Agreement shall be setteled amicably by the Parties through mutual negotiations. Any  unsettled  dispute  or  difference  shall  be  referred  to  Arbitration  by  a  Sole Arbitrator. The Arbitration shall be conducted in accordance with the rules and procedure  of  UNCITRAL  in  force  on  the  date  of  Agreement.

dd5fcc46-cf09-4f95-83ee-1b73e2f568c8.label: text_chunk
dd5fcc46-cf09-4f95-83ee-1b73e2f568c8.text:
Arbitration proceedings shall be held in India and will be conducted in English. The decision of Arbitral Tribunal shall be final and binding on all Parties. Cost of Arbitrtaion shall be borne by Parties themselves unless and otherwise ordered by the Tribunal.

8.

 This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws in force in India.

9. Miscellaneous.

fa9b9e3c-a8ce-4f78-a9cd-afae2138ec89.label: text_chunk
fa9b9e3c-a8ce-4f78-a9cd-afae2138ec89.text:
a) Except in the event of an amalgamation or merger with or takeover by a third party  of  their  business,

  neither  Party  may  assign  or  transfer  its  rights  or obligations in this Agreement without the prior written consent of the other.

b) The Parties do not intend that any agency or partnership relationship be created by them by this Agreement.

898ebd2b-6178-4c08-ba58-3ae548972790.label: text_chunk
898ebd2b-6178-4c08-ba58-3ae548972790.text: c) All additions or modifications to this Agreement must be made in writing and signed by an authorized representative of each Party.

community_summary.0:
**Summary of Relationships Involving the Discloser and Recipient**

1. **Discloser and Recipient**: The Discloser shares confidential information with the Recipient under a Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA), establishing a relationship of trust and confidentiality. The Recipient is required to cooperate with the Discloser during audits if any violations of the Agreement are suspected.

2. **Discloser and Confidential Information**: The Discloser possesses and controls the Confidential Information, indicating ownership and responsibility for its protection. The Discloser has the authority to deny disclosure of this information to safeguard its security interests.

3. **Discloser and Agreement**: The Discloser is a party to the Agreement, which governs the relationship and outlines rights such as seeking injunctive relief and conducting audits. The Agreement emphasizes the importance of protecting sensitive data.

4. **Custody and Protection**: The Discloser retains custody of specific sensitive materials, such as layout plans, ensuring they are always in their possession. The Confidential Information provided by the Discloser requires protection under the terms of the Agreement, highlighting the critical nature of safeguarding this data.

Overall, the relationships illustrate a framework of confidentiality, control, and cooperation between the Discloser and Recipient, underpinned by a formal Agreement that outlines their rights and responsibilities.
community_summary.1: The Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA) is a legal contract that governs the relationship between the Discloser and the Recipient. It establishes the terms under which the Discloser shares confidential information, ensuring that the Recipient understands their obligations regarding the handling of that information. This relationship is crucial for protecting sensitive data and maintaining confidentiality in various business interactions.
community_summary.2:
The relationships outlined involve a central entity, the Recipient, and their interactions with the Discloser, as well as their responsibilities regarding Confidential Information and a Mission/Post. 

1. **Recipient and Discloser**: The Recipient has access to layout plans provided by the Discloser, which is essential for fulfilling contractual obligations. The Recipient formally acknowledges the terms set by the Discloser concerning the handling of Confidential Information, indicating a recognition of the Discloser's concerns.

2. **Recipient and Confidential Information**: The Recipient receives Confidential Information from the Discloser and is tasked with its protection. This includes an obligation to take necessary measures to prevent unauthorized access or disclosure of this information.

3. **Recipient and Mission/Post**: The Recipient is identified as the individual or entity that receives communications related to a specific Mission/Post, highlighting their role in the operational context.

Overall, the relationships emphasize the Recipient's access to critical information and their responsibilities to protect it, as well as their role in communication related to specific missions, all under the guidance and terms set by the Discloser.

community_summary.5:
The relationships outlined involve the entities "Parties," "Sole Arbitrator," and "Arbitral Tribunal," highlighting key aspects of arbitration agreements. 

1. **Parties and Sole Arbitrator**: In the event of a dispute, the Parties may refer the matter to a Sole Arbitrator for resolution, as specified in their agreement. This indicates that the Sole Arbitrator serves as a designated figure for resolving disputes between the Parties.

2. **Parties and Arbitral Tribunal**: The Parties are bound by the decisions made by the Arbitral Tribunal, which emphasizes that the tribunal's rulings are final and enforceable. This relationship underscores the authority of the Arbitral Tribunal in the arbitration process.

Overall, the summary illustrates that the Parties have the option to resolve disputes through a Sole Arbitrator, and they are obligated to adhere to the final decisions rendered by the Arbitral Tribunal, ensuring a structured and binding resolution process.

community_summary.18:
The relationships outlined involve the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party, and Critical Energy Infrastructure Information (CEII). 

1. **Disclosing Party and CEII**: The Disclosing Party is responsible for providing CEII to the Receiving Party. This establishes a relationship characterized by the Disclosing Party's control over the sensitive information, ensuring that it is disseminated appropriately.

2. **Disclosing Party and Receiving Party**: The Disclosing Party shares information directly with the Receiving Party. This indicates a straightforward relationship where the Disclosing Party actively furnishes information, reinforcing the notion of control and responsibility in the information-sharing process.

Overall, the relationships emphasize the Disclosing Party's role as the provider and controller of sensitive information (CEII), while the Receiving Party is positioned as the recipient of this information.
community_summary.19:
The relationships outlined involve the entity "Receiving Parhall" and its interactions with "CEII" (Critical Energy Infrastructure Information) and "third party." 

1. **Receiving Parhall and CEII**: The Receiving Parhall is responsible for disclosing CEII, indicating a direct management role in the release of this confidential information. This relationship emphasizes the organization's authority and responsibility in handling sensitive data.

2. **Receiving Parhall and Third Party**: The Receiving Parhall must obtain consent before disclosing information to a third party. This establishes a critical relationship based on the necessity of permission for information sharing, highlighting the importance of privacy and regulatory compliance in the management of confidential information.

Overall, the summary of these relationships underscores the Receiving Parhall's dual role in both managing the disclosure of sensitive information and ensuring that proper consent is obtained before sharing it with external entities.
